risks.title: AI model usage without governance framework
risks.remediation: Establish an AI governance policy covering model selection, approval, monitoring, and deprecation. Create a registry of all AI models: provider, version, purpose, and data flows. Consolidate provider management. Establish per-provider risk assessments. Pin model versions in code. Establish change management for model upgrades. Audit orchestration framework dependencies. Pin versions. Monitor security advisories. Establish bias testing procedures. Monitor for disparate impact. Implement model performance monitoring: latency, error rates, quality metrics. Establish formal AI risk assessment: classify by risk level, prioritize remediation. Monitor output distributions, establish baselines, configure drift alerts.

risks.title: No documentation of AI system purposes and limitations
risks.remediation: Document purpose, capabilities, and limitations of each AI integration. Implement clear disclosure when content is AI-generated. Update terms of service. Log AI model inputs (sanitized), outputs, and decision rationale.

risks.title: No designated AI risk owner or oversight role
risks.remediation: Assign an AI risk owner for governance, monitoring, and incident response. Develop an AI-specific incident response plan covering model failures and data breaches. Implement human-in-the-loop controls for high-stakes AI decisions.

risks.title: Customer data sent to external AI provider without DPA
risks.remediation: Verify current DPA status with each AI provider. Where missing, execute a DPA. Implement data anonymization before API calls. Identify data residency requirements. Confirm provider processing locations. Implement Standard Contractual Clauses where needed. Review AWS Bedrock terms, shared responsibility model, and compliance certifications.

risks.title: No input validation before AI model calls
risks.remediation: Implement prompt injection defenses. Validate and sanitize all user input before passing to AI models. Validate and sanitize AI model outputs. Implement guardrails for harmful content. Assess whether AI use cases fall under EU AI Act high-risk categories.

risks.title: API key management — secrets manager detected
risks.remediation: Move all API keys to a secrets manager. Rotate any keys found in code. Add secret scanning to CI/CD. Review AI dev tool data transmission policies. Establish approved tool list.

risks.title: No data classification policy for AI-processed information
risks.remediation: Create a data classification scheme. Label data before it enters AI pipelines. Block sensitive/PII categories from external models.

risks.title: AI provider may retain prompts for training
risks.remediation: Review each provider's data retention policy. Opt out of training data usage where possible.

risks.title: US Entity List violation — prohibited Chinese AI provider
risks.remediation: IMMEDIATE ACTION: Provider is on the US BIS Entity List. Remove all integrations. Revoke API keys. Document removal. Consult export compliance counsel.

risks.title: Chinese AI provider — data sovereignty risk
risks.remediation: Data sent to these providers is stored on Chinese servers subject to Chinese law. Evaluate data classification requirements. Chinese AI providers do not offer GDPR/CCPA-compatible DPAs.

risks.title: Agentic AI framework without human oversight controls
risks.remediation: Implement HITL checkpoints, approval gates before external actions, and kill switches. Implement least-privilege tool permissions, sandboxing, and audit logging. Implement step-level logging with agent identity, decision rationale, and tool calls. Implement auth on MCP endpoints, restrict tool access, log invocations, establish approval gates.
